edges.desc: The party is present on Stormwreck Isle.
The party is present in the Forgotten Realms.
The party is present in the Dungeon Master's context.
serves as the characters' home base during the adventure
explore an island that has long been a battlefield in that conflict
each player chooses one character to play
Stormwreck Isle is located in the Sword Coast region
Neverwinter is located in the Sword Coast region
Mount Hotenow is located in the Sword Coast region
players can control one or more characters
Runara established the cloister of Dragon's Rest as a safe haven from violence.
Runara resides on Stormwreck Isle, where she serves as the leader of a group of hermits and ascetics.
Runara serves as the leader of a tiny group of hermits and ascetics, which includes the party.
Dragon's Rest is located on Stormwreck Isle.
Runara's presence on Stormwreck Isle is a constant reminder of the cost of conflict.
Runara serves as the leader of the cloister at Dragon's Rest.
Stormwreck Isle bears the scars of dragon conflicts, which Runara is devoted to healing.
The characters are involved in a conflict with Runara, who is a dragon and a key antagonist in the adventure.
Runara killed a blue dragon at the Clifftop Observatory, which is now the lair of the blue dragon's grandson.
The characters explore Dragon's Rest, which is part of the adventure.
The characters explore Seagrow Caves, which is part of the adventure.
The characters explore Cursed Shipwreck, which is part of the adventure.
The characters explore Clifftop Observatory, which is part of the adventure.
the characters arrive at the location marked on the map
the characters use the player version map to navigate
receives information from the Dungeon Master about the game world
The characters engage the fire snake in combat.
The fire snake is immune to fire damage from the flaming sphere spell.
The characters interact with Runara, who is described as wise and peace-loving but can be roleplayed with improvisation.
consults Appendix A to determine the properties and rules for magic items
The party encounters the Merrow Extortionist during their voyage to Stormwreck Isle.
departed from the bustling port of Neverwinter a few days ago
heading for an island with the foreboding name of Stormwreck Isle
dark clouds overhead threaten a violent storm
The party is present at the shipwreck
A monster attacks the party on the shipwreck
The party can use Charisma checks to reduce the extortion amount
The Merrow Extortionist reports to the Scaled Queen
The Scaled Queen is the ruler of the Merrow Extortionist
The party's characters attack the Merrow Extortionist in combat.
The Merrow Extortionist can be defeated by the party in 1–2 rounds.
The party is considering creative strategies to deal with the merrow.
The characters have dealt with the Merrow Extortionist, one way or another.
The party is on a ship in the sea air, continuing their journey after encountering a merrow.
The characters are at Dragon's Rest
Dragon's Rest is associated with the dangers of Stormwreck Isle
The characters seek Runara's wisdom and assistance
the party is moving towards the cloister
the party uses map 2 to understand the cloister layout
arrive at the island and begin exploring
approach the temple on the cliffside
the party's journey began from the shipwreck
the characters face a choice to fight or leave the zombies
zombies inhabit the shipwreck site
The party is at the cove, about to leave the beach.
The party hears a ruckus from the shipwreck area.
The party is present in the cove.
The shipwreck is adjacent to the cove.
the cleric's sacred flame cantrip deals radiant damage to zombies
comes to their rescue when zombies defeat the adventurers
wake up in the temple (area A5 in Dragon's Rest)
arrives just in time to prevent zombies from dragging the characters into the sea
the characters will have another opportunity to deal with the zombies later (see 'Cloister Quests' later in this chapter)
the characters escape from the zombies and head toward Dragon's Rest
Elder Runara welcomes the characters and offers them hospitality at Dragon's Rest
The characters can purchase equipment from Myla at Dragon's Rest
Elder Runara is the leader of Dragon's Rest
Myla resides at Dragon's Rest
The party is interacting with the kobolds in the cloister
The kobolds inhabit the cloister
The cloister contains the kobolds
The party is present in the cloister
The party approaches the cloister
The party approaches Dragon's Rest
The party interacts with the elderly woman in the cloister
The party interacts with the elderly woman in Dragon's Rest
The cloister is part of Dragon's Rest
guides the residents of the cloister in their contemplation and study
maintains a secret lair in a cave accessed by an undersea tunnel
connects the undersea tunnel to the cave
Tarak tends the cloister's garden plots and resides there.
Tarak frequently visits the sea caves to acquire heart cap mushrooms.
The Potion of Healing is made by Tarak in the cloister's library.
Tarak's tattoos are linked to the Gilded Gallows, a thieves' guild.
Tarak interacts with the party, initially friendly but hostile if questioned about his past.
spends her twilight years in reflective contemplation
uses mason's tools to maintain the temple and other areas
observes a ship change course and crash into the rocks to the north
can shift Varnoth's attitude to friendly through conversation on history, ethics, and impact of actions
The party is present in the map 2 area
The party is present in the player version of the map
the party ascends the path to the observatory
the stone statue is located in the plaza
the statue depicts a dragon
the plaza is part of the path leading to the observatory
characters ask Runara about the statue
Runara mentions Astalagan, who died on the cliffs
Astalagan died on the cliffs
dragon statue is located on the cliffs
Runara is connected to the dragon statue
The Characters / Party can occupy the vacant cell in the cloister.
Tarak resides in the cloister.
Varnoth resides in the cloister.
Myla resides in the cloister.
The Characters / Party may access the cloister library.
The Characters / Party may access the cloister quests.
uses the winch to haul goods up and down
the winch house is connected to the dragon's rest
the winch house is part of the dragon's rest
the party approaches the small, free-standing building halfway up the path
Tarak is the favorite cook among the party members
Stormwreck Isle is connected to the cursed shipwreck through the dining room.
spends nearly half her time studying, copying, and annotating the collection
comes here to read and discuss various works
comes here to read and discuss various works
visits for the quiet; described as studious
the party is present in the library
spends about half her time here, tending and maintaining the temple
residents of the cloister help her, most often Varnoth
the spell reveals a faint aura around the statue
a character casts detect magic in the temple
Runara is the only inhabitant of Dragon's Rest who knows that this is a lingering effect of the death of a dragon on this site
The temple is located at the highest point of the cloister
The party is present in the temple
adventurers and dragons pray to Bahamut for honor and justice
The characters have an opportunity to fight the zombies
The characters save Blepp the kobold from zombies
The characters save Tarak the human gardener from zombies
Runara suspects the shipwreck is the source of the zombies
the party is near the shipwreck as they flee from it
the shipwreck is the source of the bloated corpses chasing the party
Tarak wants to reestablish contact with the myconids of the sea caves
The characters are tasked to visit the caves
The characters are tasked to find out what's wrong with the myconids
The characters are tasked to bring back some heart cap mushrooms
Tarak warns about the fungal octopus guardian
Tarak gives the characters two Potion of Healing
Tarak gives the characters a foul-smelling sack of food scraps as a gesture of friendship
The characters are asked by Varnoth and Rix about the shipwreck
The characters are advised by Runara to investigate the Compass Rose wreck
Varnoth and Rix witnessed the recent shipwreck together
Runara suggests the answer lies in the Compass Rose wreck
Compass Rose is an older wreck compared to the recent shipwreck
The shipwreck is located north of Dragon's Rest
The characters talk to Runara about their dream and quest
Runara points the characters toward the wreck of Compass Rose
Compass Rose is part of the Shipwreck location
Runara is located at the cloister.
Runara is located at Dragon's Rest.
The cloister is part of Dragon's Rest.
the paladin is disillusioned with the corruption of Neverwinter
the paladin comes to Dragon's Rest seeking rest and new resolve
Runara welcomes the character
Runara encourages the paladin to talk to Tarak
Runara encourages the paladin to talk to Varnoth
Runara encourages the paladin to spend time in the temple of Bahamut
at the end of the adventure, she asks the paladin if they have learned anything about how to live in a world plagued with such corruption
if the character has no answer, she suggests, 'Perhaps your adventures here have shown you a way to combat evil on your own terms. Perhaps other such adventures await you.'
The rogue comes to Dragon's Rest in search of a lost fortune.
Tarak is a member of the Gilded Gallows.
Tarak's last assignment for the guild was to assassinate a traitor in Elturgard.
The characters help deal with the other problems on the island
Runara promises to direct the wizard to the observatory after the characters help deal with the other problems on the island
Runara gives the characters a moonstone key to access the observatory
Runara resides at the cloister
Runara is connected to the shipwreck events
Runara is connected to the cloister quests
The characters visit the cloister
The characters visit the shipwreck
The characters visit the cloister quests
The characters receive a moonstone key from Runara
Runara transforms into a bronze wyrmling dragon.
Runara is located on Stormwreck Isle.
keeps an eye on them and can rescue them if things go badly for them on the island
awaken in the temple (area A5) if unconscious
kobolds tend to their wounds if unconscious
the characters travel between locations on the island
the characters travel between locations on the island
the characters might stumble across fantastical creatures and locations
characters are rowing around the island or making their way along the coast at sea level
fume drakes are guardians of the spring
fume drakes attack if characters attempt to gather mushrooms or enter the water
characters are rowing around the island
characters are making their way along the coast at sea level
The party is located in the cove
The magical hot spring is located in the cove
The magical hot spring is surrounded by basalt
The basalt edges are lined with mushrooms
The mushrooms produce rainbow spores
characters can regain hit points by bathing in the hot spring
life-giving magic persists at the site of the brass dragon's death
characters examine mushrooms lining the spring
wind spores are found in the magical hot spring
characters interact with wind spores by squeezing their caps
The party encounters an owlbear that is hostile but not lethal.
The owlbear resides on Stormwreck Isle, viewing the party as intruders.
The owlbear was originally part of a performing troupe that crashed at the cursed shipwreck.
Characters can attempt to obtain the whistle from the owlbear by succeeding on a Strength check.
The owlbear is wearing a whistle around its neck that can be used to calm it.
The kobolds attempt to ambush the characters while they travel around the island by land.
The winged kobold is part of the ambush group along with the regular kobolds.
The kobolds reject the peaceful teachings of Runara.
The kobolds are rivals of Runara's teachings.
The Characters / Party are attacked by angry kobolds
characters investigate Sharruth's tomb on Stormwreck Isle
the tomb of Sharruth is located within Stormwreck Isle
Seagrow Caves are connected to Stormwreck Isle
characters could explore the caves beneath the island
Seagrow Caves are connected to Stormwreck Isle
The characters come here before going to the wreck of Compass Rose
The characters may complete chapter 3, 'Cursed Shipwreck,' before coming here
The lesser restoration spell ends the poisoning effect caused by toxic fumes in the caves.
Tarak prefers using the rowboat at Dragon's Rest to reach Seagrow Caves
The rowboat is located at Dragon's Rest
Dragon's Rest is connected to Seagrow Caves via a rowboat route
The Tides Table determines the availability of the coastal route to Seagrow Caves
the characters arrive at sea level
the party observes the cliff towering above the waves
the party observes the cave mouth with swirling colors
the cliff overlooks the cave mouth
The party is located at the cliff
The cliff is adjacent to the sea air
The characters are present in Seagrow Caves and must navigate the flooded tunnels.
The tides table dictates the flooding patterns of the tunnels in Seagrow Caves.
Myconid Adults direct Sprouts to warn of intruders
Sprouts flee to Adults to warn them of intruders
Adult Myconids inhabit Seagrow Caves
Sprouts inhabit Seagrow Caves
Sinensa is the leader of the Myconids
Sinensa was last seen in Crystal Cave
Crystal Cave is the source of the foul odor that sickened Sinensa
Characters may attack Myconids if provoked
Characters can persuade Myconids with a Charisma check
Characters can mention Tarak's name or present his offering for advantage
Tarak sent an offering to the Myconids
Rapport Spores allow telepathic communication between myconids and characters
Myconids reside in the cave
Characters are present in the cave
Myconids reside in the cave
Myconids release spores to alert others within 240 feet
All myconids in the cave are within spore range of each other
Myconids in B2 and B3 move to B4 upon detecting distress spores
the spore servant octopus attacks any creatures (other than myconids) who enter the tunnel
the myconid leader created this guardian to keep outsiders away
The fungus-covered octopus monster is present in the cavern.
The party is present in the cavern and observes the glowing spores in the water.
Stirge join the battle if fighting breaks out in this area
Stirges ignore the spore servant
the party is exploring the Seagrow Caves
the sprouts serve the adults
the sprouts are in the upper cave
the adults are in the upper cave
the violet fungus are in the upper cave
the violet fungus attack the party when they enter the cave
The Characters / Party are present in the cavern.
The cavern contains a forest of mushrooms.
The cavern has a sulfur smell.
The Characters / Party are near the mushrooms.
The sulfur smell is associated with the mushrooms.
advances into the cave
The characters use ranged attacks to kill the violet fungus monsters.
The violet fungi pose a danger to the characters, especially when they move close to them.
Some violet fungi remain motionless until characters move close, then attack.
Characters scan the fungus farm, which causes the fungi to appear more dangerous.
Myconid Sprout avoid the characters and the violet fungi.
Myconid Adult move as quickly as they can to protect the sprouts.
Myconid Adults agree to accompany the characters.
Myconid Adults vouch for them with the rest of the myconid colony.
Other myconids' attitude improves to indifferent as well.
notices that many of the mushrooms are sickly, shriveled, and blotched with black patches of decay
the blight has no obvious source
the blight has no obvious source
the blight has no obvious source
The party is present in the fungus farm where heart cap mushrooms are found.
Tarak can transform heart cap mushrooms into potions of healing.
Tarak is present in the fungus farm.
If the characters are 2nd level, two Violet Fungus are added to this encounter.
flee toward area B4 when characters enter the chamber
six stirges attack characters who enter the chamber
stirges distract characters to allow sprouts to escape
The Characters / Party are present in the mushroom-like people's location.
The cave is connected to the mushroom-like people's location.
six myconid adults are present in the seagrow caves
four myconid adults are in a dream-like trance called a meld
two conscious myconid adults guard the caves and respond with violence to threats
all six myconid adults are ill and fatigued from the blight spreading through the caves
The myconid adults serve and protect Sinensa, the unconscious leader.
The myconid adults collect spores from the barrel-sized glowing red fungus.
The myconid leader tends to the sprout
The myconid leader is in conflict with the fungus-covered octopus monster
The characters possess the ruby morel

Tarak uses the ruby morel to make an elixir of health
The characters receive the elixir of health from Tarak
Fume Drake lurk in the Seagrow Caves, attacking intruders.
The Seagrow Caves are connected to the Crystal Cave through the fissure.
The Seagrow Caves are connected to the Tomb of Sharruth through the volcanic activity.
The Seagrow Caves vent noxious fumes to the Crystal Cave through the fissure.
The Crystal Cave is connected to the Tomb of Sharruth through the volcanic activity.
The Tomb of Sharruth is connected to the Elemental Plane of Fire through rifts.
The party is present in the crystal cave.
the fire snake is released into the crystal cave
the crystal cave is connected to the western fissure
the fire crystal is located within the crystal cave

The characters encounter fume drakes when they first arrive at the location.
The characters encounter a fire snake that hatches from its egg.
The fire snake and fume drakes coexist in the crystal cave, with the fire snake hatching from its egg and the fume drakes emerging from the crystals.
characters destroy the orange crystal to improve myconids' attitude
gives ruby morel and permission to keep treasure
characters return to Dragon's Rest for elixir of health
gives elixir of health in gratitude
visited before the wreck of Compass Rose
visited after Seagrow Caves

ready to visit after advancing to 3rd level
mentioned in the context of level advancement

Zombies originated from the wreck of Compass Rose.
The harpy guards the wreck of Compass Rose.
The harpy controls the cursed talisman in the ship's hold.
The harpy feeds on sailors lured to crash on the shoal.
characters heed Runara's advice to search Compass Rose for clues
characters explore Compass Rose for clues to recent shipwrecks
spore servant octopus is found in Compass Rose wrecks

referencing map 4
The party is also present in the cursed shipwreck, which has additional ominous features.
the harpy is out looking for another ship to lure onto the rocks
the harpy returns after the characters have spent some time aboard

The Compass Rose is located within the shipwreck.
The party interacts with the Compass Rose's wheel.
The party's actions attract the attention of the zombie in area C4.
the zombies are guarding the door to the captain's quarters
The cursed shipwreck is located on the stormwreck isle.
The shipwreck is located within the cursed shipwreck.
The Characters / Party have a pouch containing 50 gp
The Characters / Party have cartographer's tools
The Characters / Party have a dagger
The compass is stored in the shipwreck
If the characters are 2nd level, add a third zombie to this encounter.
The blond-haired man in the portrait is wearing merchant's fine clothes.
A character who searches the cabin and succeeds on a 10 Wisdom (Perception) check notices a floorboard in the middle of the room that is raised slightly above its neighbors.
A character who lifts the floorboard triggers a trap: a tiny dart shoots up, making an attack against the character.
The dart attack has an attack bonus of +5, deals 2 (1d4) piercing damage, and requires a 11 Constitution saving throw to avoid 3 (1d6) poison damage.
Once the trap is triggered, it does not trigger again unless a character resets it.
If the characters figure out a way to lift the floorboard from a safe distance, the dart misses.
A sack full of 200 gp rests in a small compartment beneath the raised floorboard.
The zombie is an obvious threat to the characters.
The ghoul lurks in the aft part of the hold and waits to attack the characters.
The ghoul and zombie are both threats in the shipwreck, but have different tactics.
The ghoul aims to paralyze a character and drag them away to feast on.
The ghoul is present in the aft part of the hold within the shipwreck.
The water in the shipwreck makes the entire lower deck difficult terrain.
hears a walking corpse splashing through water
uses a crowbar to pry open crates
can find a sack of cloves inside a crate
can find a fine lute with mother-of-pearl inlay
can find a spell scroll of command
the cursed shipwreck is located within the seagrow caves
characters can carry the chest to the surface before opening it
the pouch is inside the chest in the hold
three Turquoise are inside the chest in the hold
a pair of boots of elvenkind is inside the chest in the hold
casting detect magic reveals the talisman's necromancy magic
the hold is part of the stormwreck isle
the hold is part of the cursed shipwreck
Many Undead creatures (like Ghoul) worship him or seek to bargain with him in exchange for some fragment of his power over undeath.
characters are in the shipwreck
characters are in the cove

The harpies are allies of the characters but do not trust each other.
Although they're allies, the harpies don't trust each other.
A harpy initially perches on the ballista in area C2.
The harpy uses its own Luring Song.
The characters are near Dragon's Rest when they interact with Runara.
The characters are at the cursed shipwreck, where they find Aleitha's talisman and deal with the zombie problem.
the party dreams of the island and sees the light and smoke
The characters complete this chapter of the adventure and gain a level. If they visited Compass Rose before Seagrow Caves, they advance to 2nd level.
The residents of Dragon's Rest urge them to visit Seagrow Caves next.
The characters are urged by the residents of Dragon's Rest to visit Seagrow Caves next.
If the characters have already explored the caves, they advance to 3rd level and are ready to visit Clifftop Observatory in chapter 4.
See 'Lost Wyrmling' for further information.
Runara's ancient rival was a blue dragon wyrmling

Aidron was captured and imprisoned in the observatory

Aidron's hatred of chromatic dragons overrides his disagreement with Runara

Sparkrender plans a ritual to awaken the spirits of dead dragons

The stirge attack interrupted the kobolds from their assigned task.

The kobolds were arranging the dragon effigies around the metal sculpture.
If the characters ask the kobolds about these dragon effigies, Mek and Minn explain their purpose.

A character examines the journal and succeeds on a Wisdom (Perception) check to notice an arcane rune above the keyhole.
a character uses the misty step spell to cross the gap and explore alone

found in a hidden compartment in the northwest wall
found in a hidden compartment in the northwest wall
The characters can choose to fight Sparkrender directly

Sparkrender and the Bronze Dragon Wyrmling are rivals
The blue dragon is sleeping in the clifftop observatory
The characters must avoid waking the sleeping blue dragon wyrmling by using stealth.
The blue dragon wyrmling is sleeping in the area, and noise from turning statues can wake it.
The Blue Dragon Wyrmling attacks the characters if they fail to convince him not to, or if they mention Runara or Aidron.
Despite his youth, the Blue Dragon Wyrmling is a fearsome foe and loathsome villain.
If the characters defeat him, he swears vengeance and attempts to flee the island.
Characters who speak Draconic can try to convince the Blue Dragon Wyrmling not to attack by making a Charisma check.
If the characters flee, Sparkrender taunts them as they run away.
searches the star map for a constellation resembling a dragon
